disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden total darkness during a power outage removed her only functional vision, causing disorientation, a trip-and-fall on a rug, and an acute panic episode; there was a clear mechanical/environmental trigger
trigger_question_examples: What was happening right before you fell?
Can you tell me what set this off tonight?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The power went out and it was pitch black. I couldn't see anything, I got frightened and confused, tripped on the rug and went down, and then I panicked.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Long-standing, gradual, painless bilateral vision loss — blur, disabling glare, poor night vision, and loss of peripheral (side) vision (tunnel vision); known cataracts and glaucoma under specialist care
trigger_question_examples: How has your eyesight been generally?
Do you have any eye conditions you see a specialist for?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My eyes have been getting worse for years — everything's blurry, headlights blind me, and I've lost most of my side vision. The specialist says it's cataracts and glaucoma, and I use drops.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A fall occurred in a frail older adult tonight — the in-person hands-on assessment needed to exclude an occult fracture or head injury cannot be done over telehealth
trigger_question_examples: Did you hurt yourself when you fell?
Did you hit your head or land heavily anywhere?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I came down onto the hallway floor. I don't think I hit my head and nothing's really hurting badly, but I did land hard.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negatives that lower acute risk — no loss of consciousness, no significant head strike, no new one-sided weakness or speech trouble, no chest pain; the disorientation resolved once the lights returned and she is alert and oriented
trigger_question_examples: Did you black out at any point?
Any weakness, drooping or trouble speaking now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I didn't pass out. My arms and legs work fine and my speech is normal — once the lights came back I knew exactly where I was, I was just terrified.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High ongoing environmental falls risk — loose hallway rug, poor lighting and low wall/floor contrast in a person dependent on bright ambient light
trigger_question_examples: What is the lighting like where you fell?
Are there rugs or trip hazards around the home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The hallway's quite dim and there's a rug I catch my feet on. I really need strong light to see where I'm going.
